label: Vision Transformers & Perceiver-style latent models

node.label: Vision Transformers & Perceiver-style latent models
node.definition: Vision Transformers tokenize spatial inputs into patches, while Perceiver-style models use a compact latent array to absorb high-dimensional or multimodal observations.
node.why_it_matters: In this atlas graph, Vision Transformers & Perceiver-style latent models is modeled as depending on Transformers, Striding, pooling, patchification & up/downsampling and feeding downstream nodes such as Computer vision, perception & scene understanding.
examples: atlas downstream link to Computer vision, perception & scene understanding
limitations: Its inductive bias, data demand, optimization behavior, and compute profile can make it unsuitable outside the regimes where it was validated. Its direct dependencies include Transformers, Striding, pooling, patchification & up/downsampling.

dependencies.rationale: Vision Transformers & Perceiver-style latent models extends or specializes the architecture family Transformers.
Vision Transformers & Perceiver-style latent models is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Striding, pooling, patchification & up/downsampling.
In this atlas graph, Vision Transformers & Perceiver-style latent models has a `composes` dependency on Cross-attention & co-attention with `common` strength.
Vision Transformers & Perceiver-style latent models consumes, emits, or organizes information using Images, video & spatial grids.
